message:
fix(transport/aggregator): insert current symbol into buffer before gap-recovery flush

When the SymbolReorderer encounters a gap too large or a full buffer, it
previously flushed all buffered symbols and then appended the current
symbol separately — breaking chronological delivery order because the
current symbol's sequence number could sort between buffered entries.

Now the current symbol is inserted into the BTreeMap before the drain so
it participates in the sorted iteration.  The post-flush next_expected
is also updated from the actual last-drained sequence rather than only
the triggering symbol, which was off-by-one when the triggering symbol
was not the highest in the buffer.

- Insert current symbol into state.buffer before std::mem::take drain
- Update next_expected inside the drain loop to track each emitted seq
- Remove redundant in_order_deliveries counter bump (these are timeout
  deliveries, not in-order)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
